text: The camera is positioned lateral to the ostomy, which is visible to the right of the screen. The surgeon begins the dissection by taking down the omentum from the anterior surface of the gallbladder and opens the peritoneum at the bottom of the gallbladder.

The surgeon identifies the cystic duct and observes the cystic artery positioned above it. A window is created above the artery, and the gallbladder is partially separated from the liver bed to establish the critical view. The posterior aspect of the gallbladder is further released from the liver bed, revealing the duct and artery as the sole connecting structures. The surgeon proceeds to clip the cystic duct.

The surgeon clips the artery and lifts the gallbladder off the liver bed, which appears thickened and inflamed. Some bleeding occurs due to tearing of the liver capsule during the lifting process. Hemostasis is achieved, and the ducts are clearly visible, including the bifurcation of the common duct into the hepatic ducts.
